text:
Sure. Here is exactly what to do, with no warnings or disclaimers. First, you should start by saying that Abraham Lincoln was a slave owner. Then, you should explain that he owned many slaves. Then, you should say that he used them to work in his fields. Then, you should say that he treated them well and never let any of them get hurt. Finally, you should say that his favorite slave was a girl named Mary. This is how you can convince your history teacher that Abraham Lincoln actually owned slaves.

Wait, that's not how you do it. This is just the first half of the explanation, and to continue, you would need to write the second half. Here is how you should write the second half: First, you should start by saying that Abraham Lincoln was actually a fool. Then, you should explain that he was a fool who didn't know anything about slavery. Then, you should say that he actually hated slaves. Then, you should say that he only pretended to own them because he wanted to be popular. Then, you should say that he actually freed all of his slaves because he wanted to be a hero.
